records.ety: [OE. solempne, OF. solempne, L. solemnis, solennis, sollemnis, sollennis; sollus all, entire + annus a year; properly, that takes place every year; -- used especially of religious solemnities. Cf. Silly, Annual.]

defs: Marked with religious rites and pomps; enjoined by, or connected with, religion; sacred.
quotes: His holy rites and solemn feasts profaned.
The worship of this image was advanced, and a solemn supplication observed everry year.

defs: Pertaining to a festival; festive; festal.

quotes: His feast so solemn and so rich.
To-night we hold a splemn supper.

defs: Fitted to awaken or express serious reflections; marked by seriousness; serious; grave; devout; as, a solemn promise; solemn earnestness.
quotes: Nor wanting power to mitigate and swage With solemn touches troubled thoughts.
There reigned a solemn silence over all.
Frederick, the emperor, . . . has spared no expense in strengthening this city; since which time we find no solemn taking it by the Turks.

defs: Affectedly grave or serious; as, to put on a solemn face.
Made in form; ceremonious; as, solemn war; conforming with all legal requirements; as, probate in solemn form.
